linting.notes: Ruff is configured in pyproject.toml but NOT executed in CI workflows (gap). Bandit runs in sdl.yml workflow as a gating check.

setup_required: Build C++ extension (requires OpenVINO developer package and CMake)
testing.mock_patterns: Uses parametrized tests with HuggingFace tokenizer models
testing.notes: Tests require the C++ extension to be built (BUILD_CPP_EXTENSION=ON). CI builds the extension using OpenVINO developer package. Many tests will fail/skip without the C++ extension. Uses pytest-xdist for parallel execution in CI. Some tests also validate against TensorFlow layer tests from the OpenVINO repo.

ci.notes: CI workflow is complex: downloads prebuilt OpenVINO packages from artifacts, builds C++ extension with CMake, builds Python wheel, then runs tests. Uses Poetry for dependency management. Tests run on ubuntu-22.04 runners (not in containers for test stage). Build stages use custom Docker images from openvinogithubactions.azurecr.io.

build.notes: Hybrid Python/C++ project. Uses py-build-cmake to build C++ extension via CMake. Requires OpenVINO (runtime and developer package). C++ extension is optional (BUILD_CPP_EXTENSION=ON/OFF). For development without C++ extension: pip install -e . works but tests will fail. Full build requires: cmake, OpenVINO developer package, and system build tools (gcc/clang).

conventions.import_style: Absolute imports with 2 lines after imports (isort configured in ruff)
conventions.notes: Python code in python/openvino_tokenizers/. Tests in tests/. C++ source in src/. JavaScript in js/. Ruff configured with line-length=119, ignores=['C901', 'E501', 'E741', 'W605'].

setup_commands: apt-get update && apt-get install -y git cmake build-essential curl

lint_commands.notes: PASS - All checks passed (0 errors, 0 warnings)
PASS - No security issues identified (3430 lines scanned)

test_commands.notes: PARTIAL - Tests run but many fail/skip without C++ extension. Framework works correctly. 17343 tests collected, but most require the compiled C++ extension to pass.

container_recipe.notes: Linting fully validated and works. Tests require the C++ extension to be built with OpenVINO developer package, which is complex to set up. The CI downloads prebuilt OpenVINO artifacts. For full test validation, would need to either: (1) download OpenVINO developer package tarball and build C++ extension, or (2) use CI-built wheel artifacts.

README documents 'pytest .' but CI actually uses 'poetry run pytest tests'
